text.title: Subscription Lifecycle Edge Cases Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Subscription Lifecycle Edge Cases. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Runbook covers ALL 8 cases; missing one is a gap.
rule.rationale: Runbook covers ALL 8 cases; missing one is a gap.

rule.statement: Each case has a tested webhook handler with idempotent retries.
rule.rationale: Each case has a tested webhook handler with idempotent retries.

rule.statement: Failed-renewal dunning is exactly 3 attempts over 14 days; then cancel.
rule.rationale: Failed-renewal dunning is exactly 3 attempts over 14 days; then cancel.

rule.statement: EU VAT handled via Stripe Tax (not bespoke math).
rule.rationale: EU VAT handled via Stripe Tax (not bespoke math).

rule.statement: Refund policy is named (none / pro-rated / full) and matches the legal pack.
rule.rationale: Refund policy is named (none / pro-rated / full) and matches the legal pack.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-subscription-lifecycle-edge-cases.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
